name: nyl

metadata.name: nyl
metadata.summary: Advanced templating engine for Kubernetes and GitOps.

documentation:
# nyl

Nyl is a versatile tool for generating Kubernetes manifests from a simple YAML configuration, encouraging
consistent and reusable deployment configurations, project layouts and operational workflows.

## Installation

Requires Python 3.11 or newer.

    $ uvx nyl

For some features, additional programs must be available:

* [kubectl](https://kubernetes.io/de/docs/reference/kubectl/) for most operations
* [kyverno](https://kyverno.io/docs/kyverno-cli/) when using the Nyl `PostProcessor` resource
* [sops](https://github.com/getsops/sops) when using the SOPS secrets provider

You can install Nyl with Nix to ensure all dependencies are met.

## Local development

Install the project with [Uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/).

    $ uv sync

To lint, run

    $ nix flake check

And to format code, run

    $ nix fmt

## Tracking upstream information

* Discussion around ArgoCD supporting Helm lookups (maybe with Project-level service account?), see
  https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/5202#issuecomment-2088810772

class_variables.name: nyl.generator.helmchart.HelmChartGenerator.search_path

documentation: The working directory to consider relative paths relative to.

class_variables.name: nyl.generator.helmchart.HelmChartGenerator.kube_version
documentation:
The Kubernetes API version to generate manifests for. This must be known for Helm cluster feature detection (such
as, for example, picking the right apiVersion for Ingress resources).

class_variables.name: nyl.generator.helmchart.HelmChartGenerator.api_versions
documentation:
A set of `Capabilities.APIVersions` to pass to the `helm template` function. This must be a complete set to ensure
the latest capabilities can be used by the Helm chart. Helm would usually look this up automatically with the
`--validate` flag.

classes.name: nyl.generator.dispatch.DispatchingGenerator

methods.name: nyl.generator.dispatch.DispatchingGenerator.default
documentation:
Create a new DispatchingGenerator with the default set of generators.

Args:
    cache_dir: A directory where caches can be stored.
    search_path: A list of directories to search for Helm charts in if the chart path is not explicitly
                 absolute or relative.
    components_path: Path to search for Nyl components.
    working_dir: The working directory to consider relative paths relative to.
    client: The Kubernetes API client to use for interacting with the Kubernetes API.
    kube_version: The Kubernetes API version to generate manifests for. If not specified, the version will be
                  determined from the Kubernetes API server.
    kube_api_versions: The Kubernetes API versions supported by the cluster. If not specified, the versions
                       will be determined from the Kubernetes API server.

methods.name: nyl.generator.dispatch.DispatchingGenerator.generate
documentation: Generator implementation

class_variables.name: nyl.generator.dispatch.DispatchingGenerator.kube_version
documentation: The Kubernetes API version.

class_variables.name: nyl.generator.dispatch.DispatchingGenerator.generators
documentation: Collection of generators to dispatch to based on the resource kind.

class_variables.name: nyl.generator.dispatch.DispatchingGenerator.fallback
documentation: Generator to use for any apiVersions that don't match `NylResource` (e.g. for 'Nyl components').

Dispatches to the appropriate generator based on the resource type.

Any resources can be passed to this generator, but only resources that have a corresponding generator will be
processed. Any other resources will be returned as-is.

modules.name: nyl.generator
documentation: This package contains everything related to the generation of Kubernetes manifests via Nyl.

functions.name: nyl.generator.reconcile_generator
documentation:
Recursively reconcile all Nyl resources in the manifests using the given generator.

Args:
    generator: The generator to use for generating manifests from Nyl resources.
    manifests: The list of manifests to reconcile.
    on_generated: A callback to call on each generated manifest, giving the opportunity to modify it.
                  This is used to pass the manifest through the `structured-templates` engine.
    skip_resources: A list of Nyl resources to ignore.

methods.name: nyl.generator.Generator.generate
documentation: Evaluate a Nyl resource and return a list of the generated Kubernetes manifests.
Base class for generating Kubernetes manifests from Nyl resources.

functions.name: nyl.generator.dispatch_test.test__DispatchingGenerator__default__creates_generator_for_every_nyl_inline_resource_kind
documentation:
This test ensures that the default DispatchingGenerator is created with a Generator for every Nyl inline resource
kind to ensure that all resources can be generated.

Path to the chart in the Git repository; or relative to the file that defines the resource. This path is
searched in the [`Project`] search path.

class_variables.name: nyl.resources.helmchart.ChartRef.git
documentation: URL to a Git repository containing the chart. May include a query string to specify a `ref` or `rev`.

class_variables.name: nyl.resources.helmchart.ChartRef.repository

documentation: The name of the chart. This is only needed when `repository` is set.

class_variables.name: nyl.resources.helmchart.ChartRef.version
documentation: The version of the chart. This is only needed when `repository` is set.
Represents a reference to a Helm chart.

classes.name: nyl.resources.helmchart.ReleaseMetadata

class_variables.name: nyl.resources.helmchart.ReleaseMetadata.name
documentation: The name of the release. If not set, the name of the Helm chart resource is used.

class_variables.name: nyl.resources.helmchart.ReleaseMetadata.namespace
documentation: The namespace where the release should be installed.
Metadata for a Helm release.

class_variables.name: nyl.resources.helmchart.ChartOptions.additionalArgs
documentation: Escape hatch for passing arbitrary arguments to `helm template`.

class_variables.name: nyl.resources.helmchart.ChartOptions.noHooks
documentation:
If set to `True`, oass the `--no-hooks` option to `helm template`.

When rendering templates with Nyl, just like ArgoCD, it cannot make Helm aware of a previous installation. To
quote the ArgoCD documentation:

> Argo CD cannot know if it is running a first-time "install" or an "upgrade" - every operation is a "sync'.
> This means that, by default, apps that have pre-install and pre-upgrade will have those hooks run at the same
> time.

class_variables.name: nyl.resources.helmchart.HelmChartSpec.options

classes.name: nyl.resources.helmchart.HelmChart

class_variables.name: nyl.resources.helmchart.HelmChart.metadata
documentation:
The helm chart metadata. Note that the labels and annotations are not used. The `name` and `namespae` serve as

class_variables.name: nyl.resources.helmchart.HelmChart.spec
documentation: The Helm chart specification.
Represents a Helm chart.

class_variables.name: nyl.resources.postprocessor.KyvernoSpec.policyFiles
documentation:
A list of Kyverno policy filenames, either relative to the file that defined the #PostProcessor resource
or discoverable in the project search path.

class_variables.name: nyl.resources.postprocessor.KyvernoSpec.inlinePolicies
documentation:
A mapping of policy name to the Kyverno policy document. Allows specifying Kyverno policies to be applied
to the generated manifests inline.

class_variables.name: nyl.resources.postprocessor.PostProcessorSpec.kyverno
documentation: Apply Kyverno policies.

classes.name: nyl.resources.postprocessor.PostProcessor

methods.name: nyl.resources.postprocessor.PostProcessor.process
documentation: Post-process the given manifests.

Configuration for post-processing Kubernetes manifests in a file. Note that the post-processing is always
scoped to the file that the processor is defined in. Post processors will be applied after all inline resources
are reconciled.

modules.name: nyl.resources
documentation: This package contains Nyl's own Kubernetes-esque resources.

classes.name: nyl.resources.NylResource

methods.name: nyl.resources.NylResource.load
documentation:
Load a Nyl resource from a manifest. If called directly on `NylResource`, this will deserialize into the
appropriate subclass based on the `kind` field in the manifest. If the method is instead called on a subclass
directly, the subclass will be used to deserialize the manifest.

methods.name: nyl.resources.NylResource.maybe_load
documentation:
Maybe load the manifest into a NylResource if the `apiVersion` matches. If the resource kind is not supported,
an error will be raised. If this is called on a subclass of `NylResource`, the subclass's kind will also be
checked.

methods.name: nyl.resources.NylResource.matches
documentation:
Check if the manifest is a NylResource of the correct `apiVersion` and possibly `kind` (if called on a
`NylResource` subclass).

methods.name: nyl.resources.NylResource.dump
documentation: Dump the resource to a manifest.

class_variables.name: nyl.resources.NylResource.API_VERSION
documentation:
The API version of the resource. This is usually `inline.nyl.io/v1` for resources that are inlined by Nyl at
templating time and are not present in the final manifest, or `nyl.io/v1` for resources that are actual Kubernetes
resources.

class_variables.name: nyl.resources.NylResource.KIND
documentation: The kind identifier of the resource. If not set, this will default to the class name.
Base class for Nyl custom resources.

classes.name: nyl.resources.ObjectMetadata
documentation: Kubernetes object metadata.

modules.name: nyl.templating
documentation: Implements Nyl's variant of structured templating.

documentation: Register a global function for use in structured templates.

functions.name: nyl.templating.random_password
documentation: Generate a random password.

functions.name: nyl.templating.bcrypt

documentation: Decode base64 data and then from UTF-8.

functions.name: nyl.templating.b64encode
documentation: Encode data to base64 from UTF-8 and then to ASCII.

classes.name: nyl.templating.LookupResourceWrapper
documentation:
A wrapper for a Kubernetes resources returned by `lookup()` that permits looking up fields by `__getitem__()`
and `__getattr__()`. This wraps a `ResourceInstance` or `ResourceField`, which can later be treated by the
`NylTemplateEngine` to serialize into a dictionary when embedded into a manifest.

This class is needed because the YAML serializer will not be able to serialize the `ResourceInstance` or
`ResourceField` objects returned by `lookup()`.

classes.name: nyl.templating.LookupError
documentation: Raised when a `lookup()` call fails because the resource was not found.

classes.name: nyl.templating.NylTemplateEngine

methods.name: nyl.templating.NylTemplateEngine.as_current
documentation: Set this template engine as the current one.

Nyl's structured template engine.

Args:
    secrets: The secrets engine to make available to templated expressions.
    client: The Kubernetes API client to use for lookups.
    on_lookup_failure: What should happen on failure to perform lookups of other Kubernetes resources.

classes.name: nyl.resources.placeholder.Placeholder
documentation:
A Placeholder is a dummy custom resource that is used to represent a resource that failed to evaluate during
templating. This is useful when you want to define a manifest that has dependencies on other manifests based on
`lookup()` calls, but you want to avoid the evaluation of the manifest to fail if the lookup fails.

The placeholder can inform you that a component of your manifest is missing, and you can then decide how to handle
the situation.

Note that removal of Placeholder resources is not handled by Nyl: It will either be pruned as part of a
reconciliation by ArgoCD (if you're using that) or pruned with ApplySets. If you're not using either of those, you
will have to manually remove the Placeholder resources.

A placeholder can contain arbitrary `spec` data and usually contains fields to help you identify the missing
resource.

functions.name: nyl.tools.kubernetes.discover_kubernetes_api_versions
documentation: Discover all API versions from the given Kubernetes API client.

functions.name: nyl.tools.kubernetes.resource_locator
documentation:
Create a string that contains the apiVersion, kind, namespace and name of a Kubernetes resource formatted as

    apiVersion/kind/namespace/name

This can be used to uniquely identify a resource.

functions.name: nyl.resources.applyset.calculate_applyset_id
documentation: Calculate the ID of a Kubernetes ApplySet with the specified name.

functions.name: nyl.resources.applyset.get_canonical_resource_kind_name
documentation:
Given the apiVersion and kind of a Kubernetes resource, return the canonical name of the resource. This name can
be used to identify the resource in an ApplySet's `applyset.kubernetes.io/contains-group-kinds` annotation.

Note that according to the [reference][1], the resource name should use the plural form, but it appears that the
resource kind name is also accepted. Deriving the plural form will be difficult without querying the Kubernetes
API.

[1]: https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/labels-annotations-taints/#applyset-kubernetes-io-contains-group-kinds

Args:
    api_version: The apiVersion of the resource.
    kind: The kind of the resource.

methods.name: nyl.resources.applyset.ApplySet.id
documentation: Returns the ID of the ApplySet as it is configured in the `applyset.kubernetes.io/id` label.

methods.name: nyl.resources.applyset.ApplySet.id

methods.name: nyl.resources.applyset.ApplySet.calculate_id
documentation: Calculate the ID of the ApplySet based on the name and namespace of the ApplySet.

methods.name: nyl.resources.applyset.ApplySet.tooling
documentation: Returns the tooling that was used to apply the ApplySet.

methods.name: nyl.resources.applyset.ApplySet.tooling
documentation: Set the tooling that was used to apply the ApplySet.

methods.name: nyl.resources.applyset.ApplySet.contains_group_kinds
documentation: Returns the kinds of resources that are part of the ApplySet.

methods.name: nyl.resources.applyset.ApplySet.contains_group_kinds
documentation: Set the kinds of resources that are part of the ApplySet.

methods.name: nyl.resources.applyset.ApplySet.set_group_kinds
documentation: Set the kinds of resources that are part of the ApplySet based on the specified manifests.

methods.name: nyl.resources.applyset.ApplySet.validate
documentation:
Validate the ApplySet configuration.

Mutations:
    - Sets the `applyset.kubernetes.io/id` label on the metadata of the ApplySet resource if it is not set.

Raises:
    ValueError:
        - If the resource is namespaced.
        - If the annotations has no `applyset.kubernetes.io/tooling` key.
        - If the annotations has no `applyset.kubernetes.io/contains-group-kinds` key.
        - If the `applyset.kubernetes.io/id` label has an invalid value.

methods.name: nyl.resources.applyset.ApplySet.new
documentation: Create a new ApplySet resource with the specified name.

An ApplySet functions as a grouping mechanism for a set of objects that are applied together. This is a standard
Kubernetes mechanism that needs to be implemented as a custom resource. To read more about ApplySets, check out the
following article:

    https://kubernetes.io/blog/2023/05/09/introducing-kubectl-applyset-pruning/

Nyl's ApplySet resource is not namespaces.

When loading manifests from a file, Nyl looks for an ApplySet resource to determine if the manifests are to be
associated with an ApplySet.

classes.name: nyl.tools.kvstore.JsonFileKvStore
documentation:
A key-value store that stores data in a JSON file. The file is only saved on exiting the context manager.

Supports context manager usage to acquire a file lock before reading or writing the file. If a lockfile is
used, the cached data will be discarded when the lock is released.

classes.name: nyl.tools.kvstore.SerializingStore
documentation:
Store values of a specific type in a key-value store. Values are serialized and deserialized using the
[databind.json] module.

modules.name: nyl.tools.loads

methods.name: nyl.tools.kubectl.Kubectl.diff
documentation:
Diff the given manifests against the cluster.

Args:

    on_error: What to do if the diff command fails. If "raise", raise a KubectlError. If "return", return the
        status code.
    applyset: The applyset to use for the diff. This can only be combined with the `prune` option.
    prune: Include resources that would be deleted by pruning.

functions.name: nyl.tools.fs.distance_to_cwd
documentation:
Returns the dinstance of *path* to the current working directory.

If *path* is a subdirectory of *cwd*, the distance is positive. Otherwise, it's negative. If *path* is not a
subdirectory of *cwd*, a ValueError is raised.

classes.name: nyl.secrets.kubernetes.KubernetesSecretProvider

methods.name: nyl.secrets.kubernetes.KubernetesSecretProvider.init

class_variables.name: nyl.secrets.kubernetes.KubernetesSecretProvider._api
documentation: initialized in init()

This secrets provider accesses a Kubernetes secret.

Needs to be provided with a Kubernetes #ApiClient on init.

variables.name: nyl.secrets.SecretValue
documentation: A secret is a JSON-serializable value that can be stored in a secret provider.

classes.name: nyl.secrets.SecretProvider

methods.name: nyl.secrets.SecretProvider.init
documentation:
Called after loading the provider configuration from a configuration file. The file's path is provided to
allow the provider to resolve relative paths.

Args:
    config_file: The file that the configuration is loaded from. This is useful to allow configuration
        parameters that are relative paths to be converted to absolute paths.
    dependencies: Any extraneous dependencies that may be required for the provider are passed through
        this interface, up to one per type.

methods.name: nyl.secrets.SecretProvider.keys
documentation: Return an iterator over all keys in the provider.

methods.name: nyl.secrets.SecretProvider.get
documentation:
Retrieve a secret by key.

Args:
    key: The key of the secret to retrieve.
Returns:
    The secret value.
Raises:
    KeyError: If the key does not exist.

methods.name: nyl.secrets.SecretProvider.set
documentation:
Set the value of a key.

Args:
    key: The key of the secret to set.
Raises:
    KeyError: If the key is invalid.
    ValueError: If the value is invalid.
    RuntimeError: If the key cannot be set for systematic reasons.

methods.name: nyl.secrets.SecretProvider.unset
documentation:
Unset a secret by its key.

Args:
    key: The key of the secret to unset.
A SecretProvider is a source of secrets that can be accessed by keys.

methods.name: nyl.secrets.config.SecretsConfig.load
documentation:
Load the secrets configuration from the given or the default configuration file. If the configuration file does
not exist, a [NullSecretsProvider] is used.

If no *file* is provided, and a closer \[`ProjectConfig`\] can be found, the secrets configuration from that file
is used instead, if it has any. If there is a project configuration without any secrets configuration and
a less-close secrets configuration file is found, that is used instead.

Args:
: file: A configuration file that defines zero or more :class:SecretProvider\`s. If not specified, the

  : default file will be discovered by traversin the filesystem hierarchy. If no file is found, an empty
    {class}`SecretsConfig` is returned with a {class}`NullSecretsProvider` under the `default` key.

  cwd: The working directory from which to discover the default configuration file if no *file* argument

  : is specified or is set to {const}`None`.

  dependencies: A dependency provider that is passed to {meth}`SecretProvider.init`. If not specified, it

  : will default to an empty provider, but it may cause that not all {class}`SecretProvider` implementations
    can be used.

functions.name: nyl.secrets.sops.detect_sops_format
documentation:
Tells the SOPS file format based on the file suffix. Never returns "binary".
Returns `None` if the format cannot be determined.

classes.name: nyl.secrets.sops.SopsFile

methods.name: nyl.secrets.sops.SopsFile._key2sops
documentation: Convert a dotted key to a key that SOPS understands for setting/unsetting.

methods.name: nyl.secrets.sops.SopsFile.init
documentation: SecretProvider

class_variables.name: nyl.secrets.sops.SopsFile.path
documentation:
The path to the SOPS-encrypted file. This path is resolved relative to the configuration file that the
provider is defined in.

class_variables.name: nyl.secrets.sops.SopsFile.do_not_use_in_prod_only_for_testing_sops_age_key
documentation:
The key to use for the `--age` option of SOPS. This is useful for testing purposes only and should not be used
in production.

This secrets provider decodes a SOPS-encrypted YAML or JSON file and serves the secrets stored within.

Nested structures are supported, and the provider maps them to fully qualified keys using dot notation. The
nested structure can be accessed as well, returning the full structure as a JSON object.

methods.name: nyl.profiles.tunnel.TunnelManager.open_tunnel
documentation:
Open a tunnel per the given spec. If a tunnel for the same spec already exists, is is ensures that the tunnel
state is up-to-date, which may result in restarting the tunnel. If the tunnel is broken, it is restarted.

Args:
    spec: The tunnel specification.
Returns:
    The status of the tunnel.

methods.name: nyl.profiles.tunnel.TunnelManager.close_tunnel
documentation: Close a tunnel by it's locator.

Helper class to manage SSH tunnels.

Before the tunnel manager can be used, its context manager must be entered to lock the global state.

classes.name: nyl.profiles.ProfileManager

methods.name: nyl.profiles.ProfileManager.activate_profile
documentation:
Ensure that the Kubernetes config and tunnel (if any) for the profile are available.

Args:
    profile_name: The name of the profile to activate.
    update_process_env: If `True`, update the `KUBECONFIG` environment variable of the current process to
                        point to the activated profile's Kubeconfig file.

methods.name: nyl.profiles.ProfileManager.load
documentation:
Load the profile manager from the default configuration file.

Args:
    required: Passed on to [`ProfileConfig.load()`]. If it is set to `False`, the Nyl state directory
        is assumed relative to the current working directory (`./.nyl`).

This class combines the [TunnelManager] and [KubeconfigManager] to provide a holistic interface for managing
connections and configuration to Kubernetes clusters.

functions.name: nyl.profiles.kubeconfig._trim_to_context
documentation: Trim a Kubeconfig down to a single context. If *context* is `None`, it will be trinmed to the current context.

classes.name: nyl.profiles.kubeconfig.KubeconfigManager

methods.name: nyl.profiles.kubeconfig.KubeconfigManager.get_raw_kubeconfig
documentation:
Return the Kubeconfig file for a profile.

For a `LocalKubeconfig`, the Kubeconfig file is expected to be in the default location or a custom path
specified in the environment and the path to it will be returned. For a `KubeconfigFromSsh`, the Kubeconfig
file is fetched via SSH.

Args:
    profile_name: The name of the Kubernetes profile.
    source: The source of the Kubeconfig file.
    force_refresh: If `True`, refresh the Kubeconfig file even if it is already cached.
Returns:
    The path to the Kubeconfig file and the API host and port.

Helper class to manage Kubernetes configuration files for profiles.

Args:
    cwd: The working directory for the Kubeconfig manager. This is used to resolve relative paths.
    state_dir: The directory where the Kubeconfig files are stored. Usually `.nyl/profiles`.

classes.name: nyl.profiles.config.Profile

class_variables.name: nyl.profiles.config.Profile.kubeconfig
documentation: Describe how the Kubeconfig is to be obtained.

class_variables.name: nyl.profiles.config.Profile.tunnel
documentation: Describe how to create an SSH tunnel to reach the Kubernetes cluster API.
Represents a Kubernetes connection profile.

classes.name: nyl.profiles.config.LocalKubeconfig

class_variables.name: nyl.profiles.config.LocalKubeconfig.path
documentation:
Path to the Kubernetes configuration file. Relative to the profile configuration file. If not specified, it falls
back to the default location (per `KUBECONFIG` or otherwise `~/.kube/config`).

class_variables.name: nyl.profiles.config.LocalKubeconfig.context
documentation: The context to use from the Kubeconfig file. If not specified, the current context is used.
Use the local Kubeconfig file, either from the default location or a custom path specified in the environment.

classes.name: nyl.profiles.config.KubeconfigFromSsh

class_variables.name: nyl.profiles.config.KubeconfigFromSsh.user
documentation: The username to connect to the remote host with.

class_variables.name: nyl.profiles.config.KubeconfigFromSsh.sudo
documentation: Use `sudo cat` to retrieve the file instead of ust `cat`.

class_variables.name: nyl.profiles.config.KubeconfigFromSsh.host
documentation: The remote host to connect to.

class_variables.name: nyl.profiles.config.KubeconfigFromSsh.port
documentation: SSH port to use.

class_variables.name: nyl.profiles.config.KubeconfigFromSsh.identity_file
documentation: An SSH private key file to use for authentication.

class_variables.name: nyl.profiles.config.KubeconfigFromSsh.path
documentation: The path where the Kubeconfig can be retrieved from.

class_variables.name: nyl.profiles.config.KubeconfigFromSsh.context
documentation: The context to use from the Kubeconfig file. If not specified, the current context is used.

class_variables.name: nyl.profiles.config.KubeconfigFromSsh.replace_apiserver_hostname
documentation:
Replace the hostname in the apiserver configuration of the Kubeconfig. This is useful for example with K3s when
reading reading the `/etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml` file from a remote host, but the API server in that file is not
reachable from that machine.

Note that if the host in the retrieve Kubeconfig is `localhost`, `0.0.0.0` or `127.0.0.1`, it will be automatically
replaced with the specified `host` that was also SSH-ed to, unless this option is set.
Represents how to obtain the Kubeconfig from an SSH connection.

classes.name: nyl.profiles.config.SshTunnel

class_variables.name: nyl.profiles.config.SshTunnel.user
documentation: The username to connect to the remote host with.

class_variables.name: nyl.profiles.config.SshTunnel.host
documentation: The host to tunnel through.

class_variables.name: nyl.profiles.config.SshTunnel.identity_file
documentation: An SSH private key file to use for authentication.
Configuration for an SSH tunnel.

methods.name: nyl.profiles.config.ProfileConfig.load
documentation:
Load the profiles configuration from the given file or the default file. If the configuration file does not
exist, an error is raised unless *required* is set to `False`, in which case an empty configuration is
returned.

modules.name: nyl.commands.secrets
documentation: Interact with the secrets providers configured in `nyl-secrets.yaml`.

functions.name: nyl.commands.secrets.callback
documentation: Interact with the secrets providers configured in `nyl-secrets.yaml`.

functions.name: nyl.commands.secrets.list
documentation: List the keys for all secrets in the provider.

functions.name: nyl.commands.secrets.get
documentation: Get the value of a secret as JSON.

functions.name: nyl.commands.secrets.set
documentation: Set the value of a secret.

functions.name: nyl.commands.secrets.unset
documentation: Unset the value of a secret.

modules.name: nyl.commands.new
documentation: Bootstrap files for a new Nyl project or Helm chart.

functions.name: nyl.commands.new.chart
documentation: Similar to `helm create`, but generates a much simpler template.

functions.name: nyl.commands.new.component
documentation: Create the boilerplate for a Nyl component in the components directory.

modules.name: nyl.commands.profile
documentation: Interact with your Nyl profile configuration.

functions.name: nyl.commands.profile.activate
documentation:
Activate a Nyl profile.

Evaluate the stdout of this command to export the KUBECONFIG into your environment.

functions.name: nyl.commands.profile.get_kubeconfig
documentation: Similar to `nyl profile activate`, but prints only the path to the `KUBECONFIG` file.

modules.name: nyl.commands.tools
documentation: Swiss-army-knife for various common operations in conjunction with Nyl (Kubernetes, secrets management, etc).

modules.name: nyl.commands.tun
documentation: Access SSH tunnels globally managed by Nyl.

functions.name: nyl.commands.tun.status
documentation: Show the status of all tunnels.

functions.name: nyl.commands.tun.start
documentation: Open a tunnel to the cluster targeted by the profile.

functions.name: nyl.commands.tun.stop
documentation: Close all tunnels or the tunnel for a specific profile.

functions.name: nyl.commands.run.run
documentation:
Activate a Nyl profile and run a command in the context of the profile (i.e. with the same environment variables
as printed by `nyl profile activate`).

This is useful for running commands like `kubectl` without caring so much about whether the cluster connection is
defined in a `nyl-profile.yaml` file or in the global kubeconfig. With the --inherit-kubeconfig option, you can
simply name the cluster you want to use for the command and Nyl will either load the profile from the
`nyl-profiles.yaml` configuration or from the same-named context in the global kubeconfig.
app = new_typer(name="run", help=__doc__)

functions.name: nyl.commands.tools.bcrypt.bcrypt
documentation: Bcrypt-hash the specified text. If no argument is specified, read from stdin.

functions.name: nyl.commands.crds.crds
documentation: Print out the CRDs that need to be installed on a Kubernetes cluster before you can use all of Nyl's features.

modules.name: nyl.commands.tools.sops
documentation: Utilities for SOPS files.

functions.name: nyl.commands.tools.sops.re_encrypt
documentation:
Re-encrypt a SOPS file.

This should be used after updating the public keys in the `.sops.yaml` configuration to ensure that the SOPS file
is encrypted for all the specified keys.

Note that you need to be able to decrypt the SOPS file to re-encrypt it (duh).

functions.name: nyl.commands.tools.sops.export_dotenv
documentation:
A utility function to export key-value pairs from a SOPS file in dotenv format. This is useful for exporting
environment variables from a SOPS file, e.g. using Direnv.

Note that only string values are exported.

modules.name: nyl.commands
documentation:
Nyl is a flexible configuration management tool for Kubernetes resources that can be used to generate and deploy
applications directly or integrate as an ArgoCD ConfigManagementPlugin.

functions.name: nyl.commands.version
documentation: Print the version of Nyl.

classes.name: nyl.commands.ApiClientConfig
documentation: Retrieving the Kubernetes API client depends on whether in-cluster configuration should be used or not.

modules.name: nyl.commands.argocd
documentation: Helper commands for the ArgoCD integration.

functions.name: nyl.commands.argocd.discovery
documentation:
Discover whether the current working directory uses Nyl. This is when either a `nyl-project.yaml` or
`nyl-secrets.yaml` file is present in the directory or any of its parent directories.

classes.name: nyl.project.config.ProjectSettings

class_variables.name: nyl.project.config.ProjectSettings.generate_applysets
documentation:
If enabled, automatically generate an ApplySet for every template file. The applyset will be named after the
template file, unless there is namespace defined in the template file, in which case the applyset will be named
after the namespace.

class_variables.name: nyl.project.config.ProjectSettings.generate_placeholders
documentation: **Deprecated.** Will be removed in a future version. Use `on_lookup_failure` set to `CreatePlaceholder` instead.

class_variables.name: nyl.project.config.ProjectSettings.on_lookup_failure
documentation:
Can be used to tell Nyl to create a placeholder resource (`nyl.io/v1/Placeholder`) or skip a resource that was
dependant on a `lookup()` call if the lookup fails. Otherwise, the error will be propagated.

class_variables.name: nyl.project.config.ProjectSettings.components_path
documentation: Path to the directory that contains Nyl components.

class_variables.name: nyl.project.config.ProjectSettings.search_path
documentation:
Search path for additional resources used by the project. Used for example when using the `chart.path` option on a
`HelmChart` resource. Relative paths specified here are considered relative to the `nyl-project.yaml` configuration
file.
Configuration for a Nyl project that is stored in a `nyl-project.yaml` file.

classes.name: nyl.project.config.Project

class_variables.name: nyl.project.config.Project.settings
documentation: Project settings.

class_variables.name: nyl.project.config.Project.profiles
documentation: Profiles configuration. You may also configure these in a separate `nyl-profiles.yaml` file.

class_variables.name: nyl.project.config.Project.secrets
documentation: Secrets configuration. You may also configure these in a separate `nyl-secrets.yaml` file.

A project configuration file _may_ contain profiles and secret settings in addition to what we usually call project
settings. This it to simplify the configuration in a simple project, to not require up to three separate
configuration files.

However, when Nyl loads profiles and secrets configuration, the closest configuration file is used, so a closer
`nyl-profiles.yaml` or `nyl-secrets.yaml` may override the settings in the `nyl-project.yaml` file. Do note that
multiple levels of `nyl-project.yaml` files do not stack/merge.

classes.name: nyl.project.config.ProjectConfig

methods.name: nyl.project.config.ProjectConfig.find
documentation:
Find the project configuration file in the current directory or any parent directory. The configuration, if
any, can be loaded with [`ProjectConfig.load()`].

methods.name: nyl.project.config.ProjectConfig.load
documentation:
Load the project configuration from the given or the default configuration file. If the configuration file does
not exist, a default project configuration is returned.

Args:
: file: The file to loads the project configuration from. If not specified, the file is automatically

  : discovered using {meth}`ProjectConfig.find()` by traversing the filesystem hierarchy. If no file is
    found, an empty project configuration will be returned.

  dependencies: A dependency provider for the initialization of the :class:SecretProvider\`s defined in the

  : project. If not specified, an empty provider is used, but this may mean not all implementations of
    {class}`SecretProvider` can be used.

  init_secret_providers: Whether to initialize secret providers. Usually this should be left enabled, but

  : for cases where you're not interested in the providers, you can turn this off.

methods.name: nyl.project.config.ProjectConfig.load_if_has_precedence
documentation:
Load the project configuration if it takes precedence over the given configuration file and the project
fulfills the given predicate.

Args:

: over: Path to the configuration file that the project configuration may take precedence over. The

  : precedence is determined by the distance to the current working directory (the closer file takes it).
    In addition to the locality of the file, the project configuration must also satisfy the given
    predicate to have precedence.

    If this is `None`, the project configuration is always used if it is found, but it must still satisfy
    the predicate.

  cwd: The current working directory. Defaults to the current working directory.
  predicate: The predicate that the project configuration must satisfy to take precedence. This is checked

  > if the project configuration file is closer than the given configuration file.

  dependencies: See {meth}`ProjectConfig.load`.
  init_secret_providers: See {meth}`ProjectConfig.load`.

Returns:

: The project configuration if it takes precedence and satisfies the predicate, otherwise `None`.
Wrapper for the project configuration file.

functions.name: nyl.commands.template.get_profile_kubernetes_client
documentation:
Create a Kubernetes {class}`ApiClient` from the selected *profile*.

If no *profile* is specified, but the profile manager contains at least one profile, the *profile* argument will
default to the value of {data}`DEFAULT_PROFILE` (which is `"default"`). Otherwise, if no profile is selected and
none is configured, the standard Kubernetes config load takes place (i.e. try `KUBECONFIG` and then
`~/.kube/config`).

functions.name: nyl.commands.template.template
documentation: Render a package template into full Kubernetes resources.

functions.name: nyl.commands.template.load_manifests
documentation: Load all manifests from a directory.

functions.name: nyl.commands.template.is_namespace_resource
documentation: Check if a manifest is a namespace resource.

functions.name: nyl.commands.template.is_cluster_scoped_resource
documentation: Check if a manifest is a cluster scoped resource.

classes.name: nyl.commands.template.OnLookupFailure
documentation: Need an enum for typer

classes.name: nyl.commands.template.ManifestsWithSource
documentation: Represents a list of manifests loaded from a particular source file.
